subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Analysis of a Function Defined by an Integral
stimulus: The function $f$ is defined on the closed interval $[-5, 4]$. The graph of $f$ consists of three line segments and is shown in the figure. Let $g$ be the function defined by $g(x) = \displaystyle\int_{-3}^{x} f(t)\,dt$.

parts.scoring_notes: Evaluate the integral from $-3$ to $3$ using the areas of the triangles/trapezoids formed by the line segments of $f$.

required_elements: $g(3) = \displaystyle\int_{-3}^{3} f(t)\,dt = -\tfrac{3}{2}$ (computed from the areas under the graph of $f$)

parts.prompt: On what open intervals contained in $-5 < x < 4$ is the graph of $g$ both increasing and concave down? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.scoring_notes: $g$ is increasing where $g' = f > 0$; $g$ is concave down where $g'' = f' < 0$ (i.e., $f$ is decreasing).

required_elements: The graph of $g$ is increasing and concave down on $(-5, -3)$, where $f > 0$ and $f$ is decreasing

scoring_points.description: Correct reason with reference to signs of $g' = f$ and $g'' = f'$

required_elements: $g$ is increasing because $g'(x) = f(x) > 0$ on that interval, and concave down because $g''(x) = f'(x) < 0$ (i.e., $f$ is decreasing) on that interval

parts.prompt: The function $h$ is defined by $h(x) = \dfrac{g(x)}{5x}$. Find $h'(3)$.
parts.scoring_notes: Apply the quotient rule to $h(x) = g(x)/(5x)$. Recall $g'(x) = f(x)$.

parts.prompt: The function $p$ is defined by $p(x) = f(x^2 - x)$. Find the slope of the line tangent to the graph of $p$ at the point where $x = -1$.
parts.scoring_notes: Apply the chain rule: $p'(x) = f'(x^2 - x) \cdot (2x - 1)$. Evaluate at $x = -1$.

scoring_points.description: Correct evaluation of inner function and identification of $f'$

required_elements: At $x = -1$: $x^2 - x = 1 - (-1) = 2$, so need $f'(2)$ from the graph of $f$
$p'(-1) = f'(2) \cdot (2(-1) - 1) = f'(2) \cdot (-3)$; using the slope of $f$ on the appropriate segment (slope $= -2$), $p'(-1) = (-2)(-3) = 6$
